Q11.2: The T&C are written in legal language that is completely inaccessible to the average user. Most people will not know what the legal terms in the document actually mean, especially when it comes to things like liability, indemnification, class actions, waiving rights to certain legal processes, etc. Even as someone with a language-based graduate degree, it would take me a long time to actually determine the meaning of each specific clause in the agreement and how all the different clauses may interact with each other. As I'm sure is true for most people, when I encounter these types of T&C pop-ups, I usually do not read them at all, or only skim them at a level where full comprehension is not actually possible. I enter most of these engagements assuming that social media sites are collecting any data they can get their hands on and have no qualms about redistributing it to anyone if they think they can profit from it, but there is probably a lot more important information I don't know.
